title_en: Microsoft’s gaming strategy has misfired badly
title_zh: 微软的游戏战略已大打折扣
rubric_en: A supply-chain crisis for Xbox couldn’t have come at a worse time
rubric_zh: Xbox 供应链危机的来势凶猛，不会来得更坏了

paragraphs.en: IT IS NOT yet the PayPal mafia, but the Instacart matriarchy is making its mark. Not long after Fidji Simo, ex-head of the online grocery store, became Sam Altman’s product-focused sidekick at OpenAI, Asha Sharma, Instacart’s former chief operating officer, became Satya Nadella’s Ms Fix-it at Microsoft Gaming. Groceries are a tricky, low-margin business. So is Xbox—and Ms Sharma has wasted no time in getting to work. On July 6th, less than five months after becoming the division’s boss, she launched what she called the biggest reset in its 25-year history.
paragraphs.zh: 它还称不上“PayPal黑帮”，但Instacart的“母系力量”已开始展现影响力。没过多久，前在线杂货店负责人Fidji Simo成了OpenAI的萨姆·阿尔特曼“产品管家”之后，Instacart前首席运营官Asha Sharma便成了Satya Nadella在微软游戏业务里的“万能修理工”。杂货生意本就低毛利且复杂。Xbox也是如此，而Sharma没有浪费时间就开始干活。7月6日，在上任不到五个月后，她推出了自己称为该业务25年历史上“最大规模重置”的举措。

paragraphs.en: Ms Sharma has gone about her overhaul with a candour that is rare in the mealy-mouthed world of big tech. Declaring that Microsoft’s gaming arm is “not healthy”, she announced that 3,200 employees would be axed over the next 12 months, and that up to five loss-making studios would be shed. Her diagnosis makes two things clear: first, Mr Nadella’s gaming strategy has misfired badly; second, the entire console industry is in a supply-chain crisis. No hard-core gamer herself, the battles ahead will test Ms Sharma’s mettle.
paragraphs.zh: Sharma推动这次整顿的方式非常直白，这在大型科技公司的油滑辞令世界里很少见。她公开宣布微软的游戏部门“不健康”，并表示未来12个月内将裁掉3200名员工，同时剥离最多五家持续亏损的工作室。她的判断指出了两点：第一，纳德拉先生的游戏战略明显失误；第二，整个主机行业正处在供应链危机之中。她本人并非硬核玩家，接下来的斗争将检验 Sharma 的真正实力。

paragraphs.en: With Mr Nadella’s attention focused on the artificial-intelligence boom in recent years, Xbox has suffered from neglect. Under previous management, it sought to reduce its reliance on the Xbox console and focus on its multi-platform subscription service, called Game Pass, intending to become the “Netflix of gaming”. To fuel demand, Microsoft invested what insiders say was upwards of $20bn on games and studios, in addition to the $70bn-plus it spent buying Activision Blizzard, maker of “Call of Duty”, in 2023.
paragraphs.zh: 近年纳德拉先生把注意力放在人工智能热潮上，导致Xbox长期被边缘化。此前管理层下，微软试图减少对Xbox主机的依赖，转而发展其多平台订阅服务Game Pass，目标是成为“游戏界的Netflix”。为推升需求，微软在投入游戏和工作室上的资金据内部人士称超过200亿美元，此外还在2023年花费了超过700亿美元收购《使命召唤》制作方动视暴雪。

paragraphs.en: Alas, Game Pass, which was meant to have 77m members this year, has fewer than 30m. Meanwhile, the multi-platform approach has undermined Microsoft’s own console business by making content available on other platforms, such as Sony’s PlayStation, which kept its own games off Xbox. Microsoft’s quarterly gaming revenue has been in decline since last autumn. Xbox’s operating margins are a meagre 3%. It has been losing market share to Nintendo, another console-maker. Bureaucracy has ballooned; in parts of the company, Ms Sharma says, work passes through 14 layers of management. Just like a real-life Pac-Woman, she intends to chomp those down to as few as three.
paragraphs.zh: 但遗憾的是，Game Pass原本今年预计有7700万会员，如今却不足3000万。与此同时，多平台策略又反噬了微软自身的主机业务，因为该策略把内容放到了其他平台上，比如索尼的PlayStation，而后者则把自己的游戏留在了本平台，不上Xbox。微软的季度游戏收入自去年秋季以来一直在下滑，Xbox的营业利润率只有可怜的3%。它也正在向另一家主机厂任天堂丢失市场份额。官僚体系膨胀，在公司的一些部门，Sharma说，工作要经过14道管理层审批。就像现实版的“吃豆人小姐”角色，她打算把这一路径砍到仅剩三道。

paragraphs.en: Her strategy is bold. The year of lay-offs will be the biggest in Xbox’s history. Her disposal of studios will end Microsoft’s attempt to hoover up indie game-developers. Yet it is not all cost-cutting. Insiders say Ms Sharma intends to invest in “Minecraft”, a hit game that was used as a cash cow rather than a growth engine and has lost significant ground to Roblox, a stable of games that competes for youngsters’ attention. She also plans to double down on mobile gaming using untapped expertise in King, creator of “Candy Crush”, which was bought with Activision.
paragraphs.zh: 她的战略很激进。即将到来的裁员年份将成为 Xbox 历史上规模最大的裁员期。她处理工作室的做法将终结微软此前试图收编独立游戏开发商的计划。不过，这并不只是单纯的削减成本。知情人士表示，Sharma 女士计划继续投资《Minecraft》——这款曾被当作现金牛而非增长引擎的热门游戏，如今已被 Roblox 在抢占年轻用户注意力的游戏阵营中显著落后。她还计划加大移动游戏投入，利用并未充分发挥的 King 团队经验，而 King 是《Candy Crush》的创造者，随 Activision 一起被收购。

paragraphs.en: The trickiest part will be rescuing the console. When Ms Sharma took over in February she promised “the return of Xbox”. Sales of gaming hardware have long been in decline, but insiders say Ms Sharma considers Xbox users to be her core customers, spending far more on games and services than PC players.
paragraphs.zh: 最棘手的部分将是拯救这款主机。Sharma 女士在2月接任时承诺实现“Xbox 的回归”。游戏硬件销量早已长期下滑，但知情人士称，Sharma 女士认为 Xbox 用户才是她的核心客户群，他们在游戏和服务上的花费远高于 PC 玩家。

paragraphs.en: Yet as Ms Sharma tries to win them back, the ground is moving under her feet. When she took control of the business, AI-related demand for memory chips and other components had already caused costs to surge across the consumer-electronics industry. Within her first 50 days, input costs rose by 50%, a source at the company says. All three console-makers have been forced to announce price increases at a time when growth in the industry (excluding China) is sluggish.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，当 Sharma 女士试图重新赢回这些用户时，形势却在她脚下快速变化。她接管业务之时，AI 相关的内存芯片和其他组件需求已让整个消费电子行业的成本全面飙升。公司一名消息人士称，在她上任后的前50天，原材料和零部件成本上升了50%。在行业增速（不含中国）疲软之际，三家主机制造商都被迫宣布涨价。

paragraphs.en: The component crunch will have long-term consequences. Microsoft had hoped to increase production of consoles to support the eagerly awaited release of the latest version of “Grand Theft Auto”, made by Take-Two, a listed studio, which is rumoured to have cost a staggering $2bn to develop. The supply constraints will make it harder to increase production of consoles to meet the expected demand.
paragraphs.zh: 组件短缺将带来长期后果。微软原本希望增加主机产量，以配合备受期待的《Grand Theft Auto》最新版本发布，该作由上市公司 Take-Two 制作，传闻开发成本高达惊人的20亿美元。供给瓶颈将使得微软更难提升主机产量来满足预期需求。

paragraphs.en: In 2028 both Microsoft and Sony are expected to launch the next generation of devices, which could also be hampered by the supply-chain chaos. Piers Harding-Rolls of Ampere Analysis, a consultancy, says Microsoft may be hit harder, because Sony, as a consumer-electronics company, has stronger relationships with suppliers. On July 1st Sony said it would stop selling physical discs in 2028, a decision that Mr Harding-Rolls says was long in gestation but might help offset rising costs.
paragraphs.zh: 预计到2028年，微软和索尼都将推出下一代设备，而这也可能同样受到供应链混乱的影响。咨询公司Ampere Analysis的Piers Harding-Rolls表示，微软可能受损更重，因为索尼作为一家消费电子公司，与供应商的关系更为稳固。7月1日，索尼宣布将于2028年停止销售实体光盘，Harding-Rolls表示这一决定酝酿已久，但可能有助于抵消不断上升的成本。

paragraphs.en: Some think Microsoft’s best response to both the strategic blunders and the hardware crisis would be to spin off the gaming business. Gil Luria of D.A. Davidson, an investment firm, reckons that the lumpiness of revenue as a result of seven-year console cycles is better suited to private-equity investors than to public ones.
paragraphs.zh: 一些人认为，微软应对战略失误和硬件危机的最佳办法是剥离其游戏业务。投资公司D.A. Davidson的Gil Luria推测，由于七年一代主机周期造成的收入“波峰波谷”更适合私募股权投资者，而不适合公募投资者。

paragraphs.en: There are still rich seams of potential growth within Xbox that Ms Sharma will hope to mine before a final decision about its future is made. But as Ben Thompson of Stratechery, a popular newsletter, puts it, “Sometimes it’s Game Over.” ■
paragraphs.zh: 在Xbox内部仍有大量潜在增长空间，Sharma女士希望在其未来命运作出最终决策前先加以开采。但正如流行通讯《Stratechery》的Ben Thompson所言："有时就是Game Over。" ■

paragraphs.en: To track the trends shaping commerce, industry and technology, sign up to “The Bottom Line”, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter on global business.
paragraphs.zh: 要追踪塑造商业、产业和技术的趋势，请订阅《The Bottom Line》——我们每周更新、仅面向订阅者的全球商业通讯。
